title: lesquerella arctica subsp. calderi
intro: Lesquerella arctica subsp. calderi, also known as the Bering's mustard or Bering's bladdertop, is a subspecies of plant in the family Brassicaceae, native to the northern regions of North America. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lesquerella arctica subsp. calderi include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water or oil, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
